data.title: As a team lead, I can validate architect-foundation modes by running --auto and standard on Nava template projects
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Validation walkthrough comparing --auto and standard modes on Nava template test projects
**Why**: Ensures all UX improvements work end-to-end across both modes
**Scope**:
- IN: Run --auto on template project with pre-existing artifacts, run standard on fresh template, verify 4 criteria
- OUT: Automated regression testing

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, --auto completes with exactly one confirmation prompt on a test project
  ← Satisfies: "verify (a) --auto completes with exactly one confirmation prompt"
- When this story is complete, standard mode shows one batched summary on a test project
  ← Satisfies: "verify (b) standard mode shows one batched summary"
- When this story is complete, ADRs are present after both --auto and standard runs
  ← Satisfies: "verify (c) ADRs are present in both cases"
- When this story is complete, re-run on --auto project does not overwrite customized ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md
  ← Satisfies: "verify (d) re-run does not overwrite customized ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md"

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
